description: Marketplace skills to install in Cursor for Signal Lab. Go to Cursor Settings > Skills and search for each skill name, then enable it.

skills.name: nextjs-best-practices
skills.reason: Provides App Router conventions, server/client component patterns, and Next.js-specific optimization guidance. Covers areas our custom rules don't: image optimization, metadata, route handlers, caching strategies.

skills.name: shadcn-ui
skills.reason: Up-to-date component API reference and usage patterns for shadcn/ui. Our custom shadcn-form skill covers form-specific patterns, but this covers the full component library — dialogs, data tables, navigation menus, and theming.

skills.name: tailwind-css
skills.reason: Tailwind utility class reference, responsive design patterns, and CSS variable integration with shadcn themes. Prevents common mistakes like mixing Tailwind with inline styles or custom CSS.

skills.name: nestjs-best-practices
skills.reason: NestJS module organization, dependency injection patterns, guards, interceptors, pipes. Our custom nestjs-endpoint skill is specific to Signal Lab scaffold, while this covers the broader NestJS ecosystem.

skills.name: prisma-orm
skills.reason: Schema design, migration workflows, query optimization, and relation handling. Complements our prisma-patterns rule which is Signal Lab-specific (our rule says WHAT to do; this skill explains HOW Prisma works).

skills.name: docker-compose
skills.reason: Docker Compose service configuration, multi-stage builds, health checks, volume management, networking. Essential for debugging the observability stack (Prometheus, Loki, Grafana, Promtail containers).

skills.name: typescript-strict
skills.reason: TypeScript strict mode patterns, generics, utility types, discriminated unions. Prevents 'any' usage and ensures type safety across the entire monorepo.

custom_skills_fill_gaps: Custom skills cover Signal Lab-specific workflows that no marketplace skill addresses: (1) observability skill ties together prom-client + NestJS Logger + Sentry in our specific pattern; (2) nestjs-endpoint skill provides a complete scaffold template matching our project structure; (3) shadcn-form skill wires together RHF + zod + TanStack Query + shadcn in our specific data flow.
